\"2026-03-31T21:32:42-07:00\"}\n./prompts/04-docker-compose-foundation.yolopilot.jsonlines:110:{\"color\": \"cyan\", \"emoji\": \"\\ud83d\\udee0\\ufe0f\", \"event_type\": \"Command Finished\", \"message\": \"completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' README.md\\\"\", \"raw_event\": {\"item\": {\"aggregated_output\": \"# Storyteller\\n\\nStoryteller is a local-first, full-stack app for creating bedtime stories from first idea to finished text and finished narration audio. The product is session-based: the first meaningful screen is a past-sessions view where a user can resume work in progress, reopen a completed story, or start a new session.\\n\\nThis repository currently contains the first scaffold for that system. The project charter in this README and the supporting docs define the product and architecture that later prompts will implement.\\n\\n## Product Vision\\n\\nThe app should feel like a guided bedtime story studio, not a generic prompt box. A user should be able to:\\n\\n- See past sessions first and continue where they left off.\\n- Collaborate through both chat and direct UI controls.\\n- Move through a structured planning workflow before long-form writing starts.\\n- Watch story composition and audio generation progress in real time.\\n- Return later to read, listen, download, or revise completed work.\\n\\nThe tone of the product should stay calm, readable, and trustworthy. Wonder, mystery, and adventure are welcome, but bedtime suitability is a hard requirement: emotional stakes should resolve safely and the ending should feel restful.\\n\\n## Required Story Workflow\\n\\nThe application is being built around this ordered workflow:\\n\\n1. Genre selection\\n2. Tone selection\\n3. Story setup / free-form brief\\n4. Story pitches\\n5. Character sheet\\n6. Save-the-Cat beat sheet\\n7. Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters\\n8. Composition\\n9. Audio configuration and audio generation\\n10. Finalize / read / listen / download\\n\\nEach stage must be durable, resumable, and editable. Chat messages can propose actions in the workflow, and direct UI actions must also be reflected back into the session chat log as compact summaries.\\n\\n## Fixed Technical Decisions\\n\\nThese choices are not optional for this project:\\n\\n- Frontend: React + Vite + TypeScript\\n- Backend: Python + FastAPI\\n- Structured data: PostgreSQL\\n- Blob storage: file-backed GCS emulator in local development\\n- Local orchestration: Docker Compose\\n- Secrets handling: local `secrets.yaml`, never committed to git\\n- AI access: Gemini 3.1 family behind backend-owned adapters and services\\n\\nTwo architectural rules follow from those constraints:\\n\\n- The browser must never hold provider secrets or call Gemini directly.\\n- Long-running story composition and narration generation must be durable background jobs with resumable server-side state.\\n\\nMore detail is in [docs/product-brief.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/docs/product-brief.md) and [docs/architecture-overview.md](/Users/kevin/code/storyteller/docs/architecture-overview.md).\\n\\n## Local Development Expectations\\n\\nThe repo is intended to run locally with Docker Compose. At the current scaffold stage:\\n\\n- `frontend/` contains a Vite React app served on `http://localhost:8566`\\n- `backend/` contains a FastAPI app served on `http://localhost:8565`\\n- `infra/compose/` holds the canonical Compose file for local orchestration\\n- `tools/webapp-qa/` contains the browser automation container used for local UI verification\\n\\nStart the current stack with:\\n\\n```bash\\n./scripts/dev-compose.sh up --build\\n```\\n\\nThe local Compose stack now includes:\\n\\n- `frontend` on `http://localhost:8566`\\n- `backend` on `http://localhost:8565`\\n- `postgres` on `localhost:8567`\\n- `gcs` on `http://localhost:8568`\\n\\n`secrets.yaml` is already gitignored and reserved for local-only credentials. The compose file uses local development defaults for Postgres and the GCS emulator so a fresh clone can start without hand-editing configuration.\\n\\n## Docker Compose Local Stack\\n\\nUse the repository wrapper so Docker Compose always targets the canonical file under `infra/compose/`:\\n\\n```bash\\n./scripts/dev-compose.sh up --build\\n```\\n\\nStop the stack without deleting persisted data:\\n\\n```bash\\n./scripts/dev-compose.sh down\\n```\\n\\nReset the local database and file-backed object storage only when you intentionally want a clean slate:\\n\\n```bash\\n./scripts/dev-compose.sh down --volumes\\n```\\n\\nPersistent data lives in named Docker volumes:\\n\\n- `storyteller_postgres_data` for PostgreSQL\\n- `storyteller_gcs_data` for the file-backed GCS emulator\\n\\nThe backend receives the local infrastructure coordinates through environment variables in Compose:\\n\\n- `STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL=postgresql://storyteller:storyteller@postgres:5432/storyteller`\\n- `STORYTELLER_GCS_ENDPOINT=http://gcs:4443`\\n- `STORYTELLER_GCS_BUCKET_NAME=storyteller-dev`\\n- `STORYTELLER_GCS_PROJECT_ID=storyteller-local`\\n- `STORYTELLER_GCS_PUBLIC_URL=http://localhost:8568`\\n\\n## Repository Shape\\n\\n```text\\n.\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 backend/\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 app/\\n\\u2502   \\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 api/\\n\\u2502   \\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 db/\\n\\u2502   \\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 models/\\n\\u2502   \\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 services/\\n\\u2502   \\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 settings/\\n\\u2502   \\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 main.py\\n\\u2502   \\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 worker/\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 tests/\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 migrations/\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 Dockerfile\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 README.md\\n\\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 requirements.txt\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 docs/\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 frontend/\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 infra/\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 compose/\\n\\u2502   \\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 docker-compose.yml\\n\\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 persistence/\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 prompts/\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 scripts/\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 test-assets/\\n\\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 tools/\\n    \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 webapp-qa/\\n```\\n\\n- `frontend/`: browser client and TypeScript UI foundation\\n- `backend/`: FastAPI API code, settings, services, tests, worker home, and migration home\\n- `infra/`: Compose definitions and infrastructure notes\\n- `docs/`: product notes, architecture notes, and future ADRs\\n- `scripts/`: developer entrypoints such as `dev-compose.sh`\\n- `test-assets/`: reusable fixtures for UI, audio, and integration testing\\n- `prompts/`: sequential build prompts and task summaries\\n- `tools/`: local QA and developer tooling\\n\\n## What Success Looks Like\\n\\nA new engineer should be able to open this repository and quickly understand:\\n\\n- the first screen is the past-sessions home for resume, edit, and create flows\\n- the story workflow is staged and ordered\\n- the final product writes stories and generates narration audio durably\\n- the backend owns AI access, long-running jobs, and persistent state\\n\", \"command\": \"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' README.md\\\"\", \"exit_code\": 0, \"id\": \"item_58\", \"status\": \"completed\", \"type\": \"command_execution\"}, \"type\": The compose file uses local development defaults for Postgres and the GCS emulator so a fresh clone can start without hand-editing configuration.\\n+\\n+## Docker Compose Local Stack\\n+\\n+Use the repository wrapper so Docker Compose always targets the canonical file under `infra/compose/`:\\n+\\n+```bash\\n+./scripts/dev-compose.sh up --build\\n+```\\n+\\n+Stop the stack without deleting persisted data:\\n+\\n+```bash\\n+./scripts/dev-compose.sh down\\n+```\\n+\\n+Reset the local database and file-backed object storage only when you intentionally want a clean slate:\\n+\\n+```bash\\n+./scripts/dev-compose.sh down --volumes\\n+```\\n+\\n+Persistent data lives in named Docker volumes:\\n+\\n+- `storyteller_postgres_data` for PostgreSQL\\n+- `storyteller_gcs_data` for the file-backed GCS emulator\\n+\\n+The backend receives the local infrastructure coordinates through environment variables in Compose:\\n+\\n+- `STORYTELLER_DATABASE_URL=postgresql://storyteller:storyteller@postgres:5432/storyteller`\\n+- `STORYTELLER_GCS_ENDPOINT=http://gcs:4443`\\n+- `STORYTELLER_GCS_BUCKET_NAME=storyteller-dev`\\n+- t-window-foundation.md\\nprompts/60-composition-job-orchestration.md\\nprompts/96-compose-dev-and-prod-shapes.md\\nprompts/49-beat-sheet-refinement-ui-and-chat.md\\nprompts/93-accessibility-pass-and-keyboard-support.md\\nprompts/15-event-log-and-audit-trail.md\\nprompts/47-character-refinement-via-chat.md\\nprompts/16-asset-metadata-and-file-records.md\\nprompts/76-audio-preview-and-segment-status-ui.md\\nprompts/53-outline-drill-down.md\\nprompts/66-rewrite-prior-segments.md\\nprompts/18-postgres-job-runner-skeleton.md\\nprompts/43-brief-normalization-service.md\\nprompts/base_prompt.md\\nprompts/50-story-length-duration-and-chapter-setup.md\\nprompts/86-completed-session-summary-and-search.md\\nprompts/82-html-markdown-and-reader-formatting.md\\nprompts/77-final-audio-assembly.md\\nprompts/79-audio-pipeline-tests.md\\nprompts/34-ui-events-to-agent-context.md\\nprompts/89-full-journey-e2e-test.md\\nprompts/57-composition-prompt-assembly.md\\nprompts/39-bridge-and-replay-tests.md\\nprompts/03-fastapi-scaffold.md\\nprompts/31-intent-parser-service.md\\nprompts/75-audio-job-orchestration.md\\nprompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.md\\nprompts/85-audio-player-with-text-sync-hooks.md\\nprompts/05-backend-settings-and-secrets-loading.md\\nprompts/33-action-echoes-in-chat.md\\nprompts/08-repo-quality-tooling.md\\nprompts/19-data-layer-integration-tests.md\\nprompts/73-gemini-tts-integration.md\\nprompts/71-audio-length-estimation.md\\nprompts/22-workspace-two-pane-layout.md\\nprompts/38-session-replay-and-resume-hydration.md\\nprompts/10-domain-model-and-state-machine.md\\nprompts/40-genre-selection-api-and-ui.md\\nprompts/70-audio-settings-model-and-ui.md\\nprompts/69-composition-loop-e2e-test.md\\nprompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.jsonlines\\nprompts/27-reusable-form-and-card-components.md\\nprompts/98-demo-data-and-onboarding.md\\nprompts/21-past-sessions-home-screen.md\\nprompts/51-correlation-heuristics.md\\nprompts/35-chat-commands-and-quick-actions.md\\nprompts/55-continuity-bible.md\\nprompts/28-error-loading-and-feedback-states.md\\nprompts/32-ui-action-policy-engine.md\\nprompts/94-performance-pass.md\\nprompts/29-frontend-websocket-client-skeleton.md\\nprompts/58-usage-cost-and-latency-metrics.md\\nprompts/68-autosave-drafts-and-partials.md\\nprompts/09-initial-system-diagram-and-adr.md\\nprompts/91-rate-limits-retries-and-fallbacks.md\\nprompts/24-design-tokens-and-accessibility-foundation.md\\nprompts/84-reader-ui-with-navigation.md\\nprompts/59-planning-funnel-e2e-test.md\\n\", \"Command Finished\", \"message\": \"completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,220p' prompts/00-project-charter.md\\\"\", \"raw_event\": {\"item\": {\"aggregated_output\": \"# 00 \\u2014 Project Charter and Success Criteria\\n\\nRead `base_prompt.md` first and preserve all prior prompt work.\\n\\n## Objective\\n\\nCreate the initial project framing for a full-stack app that helps a user build a bedtime story, turn it into audio, and resume past sessions.\\n\\n## Build\\n- Write a top-level README that explains the product vision, the end-to-end story workflow, the required stages, and the major technical choices already fixed by the brief.\\n- Document the non-negotiable requirements: React + Vite frontend, Python FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL for all structured data, a file-backed GCS emulator for blobs, Docker Compose for local orchestration, and `secrets.yaml` kept out of git.\\n- Add a short architecture note describing why all Gemini calls must happen on the backend and why long-running composition and audio jobs need resumable server-side state.\\n\\n## Deliverables\\n\\n- `README.md` with product overview and local development expectations\\n- `docs/product-brief.md` or equivalent design note\\n- `docs/architecture-overview.md` with the first cut of the system picture\\n\\n## Acceptance checks\\n\\n- A new engineer can read the repo root and understand what is being built.\\n- The required story stages from the brief are explicitly listed in order.\\n- The first screen requirement \\u2014 a past-sessions screen for resume/edit/create \\u2014 is called out early, not buried.\\n\\n## Notes\\n\\nDo not generate implementation code yet beyond lightweight documentation scaffolding.\\n\\n## Suggested commit label\\n\\n`feat(prompt-00): project charter`\\n\", \"2026-03-31T20:56:08-07:00\"}\n./prompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.jsonlines:17:{\"color\": \"cyan\", \"emoji\": \"\\ud83d\\udee0\\ufe0f\", \"event_type\": \"Command Finished\", \"message\": \"completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,240p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\"\", \"raw_event\": {\"item\": {\"aggregated_output\": \"# Base Prompt \\u2014 Build the Bedtime Story + Audio App\\n\\nYou are building a production-minded local-first application that helps a user create a bedtime story from idea to finished text and finished narration audio.\\n\\nThis prompt pack is designed to be used **sequentially**. Read this file first, then work through the numbered prompts in order. Each numbered prompt should be treated as an incremental implementation task inside the same repository. Preserve prior work unless the new prompt explicitly asks you to refactor it.\\n\\n## Product Summary\\n\\nThe product is a guided story-creation studio with a persistent session model. A user should be able to:\\n\\n1. See past story sessions first, including both in-progress and completed work.\\n2. Open a session to resume, edit, or finalize it.\\n3. Use a **two-pane workspace**:\\n   - **Left pane:** chat window, about one-third of the desktop width.\\n   - **Main pane:** structured workflow UI, about two-thirds of the desktop width.\\n4. Control the product in **both directions**:\\n   - The user can type in chat, and the system can translate those messages into UI actions.\\n   - The user can act directly in the UI, and those actions should be reflected in the chat log and in durable session state.\\n5. Move through a staged workflow:\\n   - Genre selection\\n   - Tone selection\\n   - Story setup / free-form brief\\n   - Story pitches\\n   - Character sheet\\n   - Save-the-Cat beat sheet\\n   - Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters\\n   - Composition\\n   - Audio configuration and audio generation\\n   - Finalize / read / listen / download\\n6. Download the final story as a **Word document** and the final narration as an audio file.\\n7. Read or listen inside the product UI after generation is complete.\\n\\n## Story-Generation Philosophy\\n\\nThe app is for **bedtime stories**, not generic fiction generation. That means:\\n\\n- Wonder is welcome.\\n- Adventure is welcome.\\n- Mystery is welcome.\\n- Tension is allowed, but it should be modulated for bedtime use.\\n- Emotional repair matters.\\n- The ending should feel safe, satisfying, and restful.\\n- The narration and UI tone should remain calm, readable, and trustworthy.\\n\\nUse the research docs in this prompt pack as direct product input, especially the Save The Cat notes and the curated genre/tone catalog.\\n\\n## Required Story Workflow\\n\\nThe session should support this exact conceptual flow:\\n\\n1. **Genre Selection**\\n   - The user selects from the curated genre list.\\n2. **Tone Selection**\\n   - The user is shown preconfigured tone options for the chosen genre.\\n3. **Story Setup**\\n   - The user provides a free-form description of the story they want to tell.\\n4. **Story Pitches**\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate pitches.\\n   - The user can select one, ask for alternatives, or refine via chat.\\n5. **Character Sheet**\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate character sheets.\\n   - The user can select or refine via chat.\\n6. **Story Beats**\\n   - Gemini generates a Save-the-Cat beat sheet for the selected plan.\\n   - The user can refine the beats via chat or UI.\\n7. **Story Setup Preferences**\\n   - The user can set soft planning targets such as word count, read-aloud duration, and chapter organization.\\n   - These are **guides**, not strict constraints.\\n8. **Composition**\\n   - The system writes in segments.\\n   - The UI shows a live typewriter-like experience.\\n   - Writing should generally appear faster than the user can comfortably read.\\n   - A visible progress bar should show overall story progress.\\n   - The agent should periodically post chat summaries of what it is writing.\\n   - The user can interrupt and redirect composition, including causing rewrites of prior segments.\\n9. **Audio**\\n   - The user configures voice, music, speed, and related settings.\\n   - The system estimates final length heuristically.\\n   - Audio is generated in segments with visible progress.\\n10. **Finalize**\\n   - The user can read and listen in the UI.\\n   - The user can download the story as a Word document and the final audio as a file.\\n\\n## Non-Negotiable Technical Requirements\\n\\n### Frontend\\n- React\\n- Vite\\n- TypeScript\\n- The browser must **never** hold provider secrets\\n- Chat pane on the left, main pane on the right\\n- Past sessions screen must be the first meaningful screen\\n\\n### Backend\\n- Python\\n- FastAPI\\n- Durable backend-owned business logic\\n- Clear separation between route handlers, domain services, AI orchestration, and persistence\\n\\n### Data and Infrastructure\\n- PostgreSQL is the durable store for structured application state\\n- Use a **file-backed GCS emulator** for object/blob storage in local development\\n- The app must run with Docker Compose\\n- Postgres and object storage must both be **persistent across restarts**\\n- `secrets.yaml` exists locally for API keys and must **not** be committed to git\\n\\n### AI\\n- Use the **Gemini 3.1 family** for text/planning/composition workloads\\n- Keep model IDs centralized in config, not scattered through the codebase\\n- Put AI calls behind backend services and adapters\\n- Use structured outputs wherever determinism matters\\n- Use separate adapters for:\\n  - planning/classification/intent parsing\\n  - long-form story generation\\n  - narration / TTS\\n- Keep narration generation isolated behind a provider-style interface because model offerings evolve\\n\\n## Recommended Architecture\\n\\nUse a calm, maintainable architecture rather than a clever one.\\n\\n### Suggested application shape\\n- `frontend/` \\u2014 React + Vite app\\n- `backend/` \\u2014 FastAPI app plus domain services\\n- `infra/` \\u2014 Docker Compose, Dockerfiles, local infrastructure helpers\\n- `docs/` \\u2014 architecture notes, ADRs, product docs\\n\\n### Suggested backend layers\\n- `api/` or route handlers\\n- `settings/`\\n- `db/`\\n- `models/`\\n- `repositories/`\\n- `services/`\\n- `worker/`\\n- `ai/`\\n- `storage/`\\n\\n### Suggested durable concepts\\n- Session\\n- Workflow stage\\n- Event log\\n- Selected genre\\n- Selected tone\\n- Story brief\\n- Pitch batch + selected pitch\\n- Character batch + selected character sheet\\n- Beat sheet\\n- Story setup preferences\\n- Outline / chapter or scene plan\\n- Continuity bible\\n- Composition job\\n- Composition segment\\n- Audio job\\n- Asset record\\n\\n## Real-Time and Long-Running Work\\n\\nTreat composition and audio generation as **durable background jobs**, not request-thread work.\\n\\n- The browser should be able to refresh without losing the job.\\n- The UI should receive live progress updates.\\n- The backend should own current truth about progress.\\n- A separate worker process is strongly preferred.\\n- Use PostgreSQL-backed durable job records.\\n- Persist partial outputs frequently enough that resume is trustworthy.\\n\\n## Chat and UI Bridge Rules\\n\\nThe product must support true bidirectional interaction.\\n\\n### Chat-to-UI\\nA user message can propose actions like:\\n- select a tone\\n- regenerate pitches\\n- choose a pitch\\n- adjust character traits\\n- soften the midpoint\\n- shorten the story target\\n- pause writing\\n- change narration speed\\n\\n### UI-to-Chat\\nA direct UI action should appear in the chat history as a compact structured summary, for example:\\n- \\u201cSelected genre: Quest Fantasy\\u201d\\n- \\u201cUpdated target runtime: ~12 minutes\\u201d\\n- \\u201cAccepted revised beat sheet\\u201d\\n\\n### Safety rule\\nThe model can **propose** actions, but a deterministic policy layer should decide whether they are valid in the current session state.\\n\\n## Save The Cat Expectations\\n\\nUse the Save The Cat method as the backbone of planning.\\n\\n- The beat sheet should be explicit, editable, and stored as structured data.\\n- The composition stage should write against the beat sheet and the detailed outline derived from it.\\n- Bedtime adaptation matters: the low points can be emotionally meaningful without becoming harsh or distressing.\\n- The final story should still feel like a transformation story, not just a string of nice scenes.\\n\\n## Genre and Tone Expectations\\n\\nUse the included research docs to seed:\\n- a curated genre list\\n- per-genre tone options\\n- bedtime-suitable arc notes\\n- tone descriptors that can guide UI copy and AI prompting\\n\\nDo **not** label tones with living or copyrighted-author names. Use generic tone labels and descriptors.\\n\\n## Audio Expectations\\n\\nNarration must be treated as a real product feature, not a side demo.\\n\\n- Segment audio generation so it is resumable\\n- Estimate length heuristically before generation\\n- Support voice selection\\n- Support speed controls\\n- Support optional background music\\n- Keep music subordinate to narration clarity\\n- Produce a final compiled audio artifact\\n- Keep intermediate and final artifacts trackable through durable metadata\\n\\n## Export Expectations\\n\\nAt minimum, the user must be able to get:\\n- final story text in the app\\n- final audio in the app\\n- downloadable `.docx`\\n- downloadable final audio file\\n\\n## Developer Experience and Quality Bar\\n\\nFavor:\\n- typed interfaces\\n- boring, explicit service boundaries\\n- migrations\\n- tests around durable state\\n- visible logs\\n- simple scripts for local dev\\n\", \"command\": \"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,240p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\"\", {\"aggregated_output\": \"import { useEffect, useState } from \\\"react\\\";\\n\\nconst apiUrl = import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL || \\\"\\\";\\n\\nexport default function App() {\\n  const [message, setMessage] = useState(\\\"Loading backend message...\\\");\\n\\n  useEffect(() => {\\n    fetch(`${apiUrl}/api/hello`)\\n      .then((response) => response.json())\\n      .then((data) => setMessage(data.message))\\n      .catch(() => setMessage(\\\"Backend unavailable\\\"));\\n  }, []);\\n\\n  return (\\n    <main className=\\\"app-shell\\\">\\n      <section className=\\\"card\\\" data-testid=\\\"app-card\\\">\\n        <p className=\\\"eyebrow\\\">React Frontend</p>\\n        <h1>Hello, world!</h1>\\n        <p>This React app is running on port 8566.</p>\\n        <p className=\\\"api-message\\\" data-testid=\\\"api-message\\\">\\n          {message}\\n        </p>\\n      </section>\\n    </main>\\n  );\\n}\\n\", \"command\": \"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' \"completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,240p' docs/product-brief.md\\\"\", \"raw_event\": {\"item\": {\"aggregated_output\": \"# Product Brief\\n\\n## Product Summary\\n\\nStoryteller is a session-based bedtime story creation studio. It helps a user move from a loose story idea to a finished written story and finished narration audio, with the ability to leave and come back later without losing progress.\\n\\nThe first meaningful screen is the sessions home. It must foreground past sessions, including both in-progress and completed stories, so resume and revisit are the default behaviors rather than an afterthought.\\n\\n## User Experience Principles\\n\\n- Bedtime-first: stories should support calm endings, emotional repair, and low-friction read-aloud use.\\n- Guided, not opaque: users move through explicit workflow stages instead of one giant prompt box.\\n- Bidirectional control: chat can suggest UI actions, and UI actions must be echoed back into chat.\\n- Durable by default: progress survives refreshes, restarts, and long-running generation work.\\n- Local-first development: the full stack should run locally through Docker Compose with persistent infrastructure.\\n\\n## End-to-End Workflow\\n\\nThe product must support this ordered flow:\\n\\n1. Genre selection from a curated genre list.\\n2. Tone selection from options preconfigured for the chosen genre.\\n3. Story setup through a free-form brief describing the desired story.\\n4. Story pitches generated by Gemini, with selection, refinement, or regeneration.\\n5. Character sheet generation, again with selection and refinement.\\n6. Save-the-Cat beat sheet generation and editing.\\n7. Story setup preferences for soft targets such as word count, runtime, and chapters.\\n8. Composition in segments with visible progress, typewriter-like output, and interruptibility.\\n9. Audio configuration and audio generation, including voice and speed controls plus optional background music.\\n10. Finalize, where the user can read, listen, and download story and audio artifacts.\\n\\n## Core Product Behaviors\\n\\n### Sessions and Resume\\n\\n- Sessions are durable backend entities.\\n- A user can reopen a session from any stage.\\n- In-progress and completed sessions both appear on the home screen.\\n\\n### Workspace Layout\\n\\n- Desktop layout uses two panes.\\n- Left pane is the chat workspace, about one-third width.\\n- Right pane is the main workflow UI, about two-thirds width.\\n\\n### Chat and UI Bridge\\n\\n- User chat can propose structured actions such as selecting a tone or refining beats.\\n- The model may propose actions, but a deterministic backend policy layer decides whether each action is valid for the session's current stage.\\n- Direct UI actions must create compact chat-visible summaries for auditability and replay.\\n\\n### Composition Experience\\n\\n- Story writing happens in segments.\\n- Progress is visible and should feel meaningfully live.\\n- The system should periodically summarize what it is writing in chat.\\n- Users can interrupt, redirect, and request rewrites.\\n\\n### Audio Experience\\n\\n- Narration is a first-class feature, not a demo extra.\\n- Audio generation is segmented and resumable.\\n- The app estimates likely runtime before generation starts.\\n- Final audio should be playable in-app and downloadable.\\n\\n## Non-Negotiable Technical Constraints\\n\\n- Frontend: React, Vite, TypeScript\\n- Backend: Python, FastAPI\\n- Structured persistence: PostgreSQL\\n- Object storage in local development: file-backed GCS emulator\\n- Local orchestration: Docker Compose\\n- Secrets: local `secrets.yaml`, excluded from git\\n- AI model family: Gemini 3.1, with backend-owned adapters and centralized model configuration\\n\\n## Deliverable Expectations\\n\\nThe finished product should let a user:\\n\\n- create and revise a bedtime story plan\\n- generate and edit a complete story\\n- generate, play, and download narration audio\\n- download the story as `.docx`\\n- resume work from prior sessions without losing context\\n\", \"command\": \"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,240p' docs/product-brief.md\\\"\", \"exit_code\": 0, \"id\": \"item_20\", \"status\": \"completed\", \"type\": \"command_execution\"}, \"type\": \"item.completed\"}, \"task_display_context\": {\"event_number\": 41, \"prompt_index\": 1, \"prompt_name\": \"00 \\u2014 Project Charter and Success Criteria\", \"prompt_total\": 100}, \"timestap\": \"2026-03-31T20:57:39-07:00\"}\n./prompts/00-project-charter.yolopilot.jsonlines:42:{\"color\": \"cyan\", \"emoji\": \"\\ud83d\\udee0\\ufe0f\", \"event_type\": \"Command Finished\", \"message\": \"completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/architecture-overview.md\\\"\", \"raw_event\": {\"item\": {\"aggregated_output\": \"# Architecture Overview\\n\\n## System Picture\\n\\nStoryteller is a full-stack application with a browser client, a backend-owned workflow engine, durable relational state, durable object storage, and resumable background jobs.\\n\\n```text\\nReact + Vite client\\n        |\\n        v\\nFastAPI API layer\\n        |\\n        +--> domain services and policy layer\\n        |         |\\n        |         +--> AI adapters for planning, composition, and narration\\n        |         |\\n        |         +--> repositories for sessions, workflow state, jobs, and event log\\n        |\\n        +--> background workers for composition and audio generation\\n                  |\\n                  +--> PostgreSQL for durable structured state\\n                  +--> file-backed GCS emulator for artifacts and intermediate blobs\\n```\\n\\n## Why Gemini Calls Must Stay on the Backend\\n\\nAll Gemini access belongs on the server side for four reasons:\\n\\n1. Secrets control: provider credentials live in local `secrets.yaml` and must never enter the browser.\\n2. Policy enforcement: chat-to-UI actions and workflow transitions need deterministic server validation before the model can change session state.\\n3. Durable orchestration: prompts, structured outputs, retries, and model selection need to be coordinated with persistent session data and job records.\\n4. Provider flexibility: keeping Gemini behind backend adapters lets the app keep a stable internal interface while model IDs, structured output tactics, or narration providers evolve.\\n\\nThe frontend should only call the backend's API and subscribe to backend-owned progress events.\\n\\n## Why Composition and Audio Must Use Resumable Server-Side Jobs\\n\\nComposition and narration are long-running operations that cannot safely live inside request threads or browser memory. They need server-side jobs because:\\n\\n- a user must be able to refresh the page or return later without losing progress\\n- partial story text and audio segments need durable checkpoints\\n- progress updates need a single source of truth that both chat and UI can observe\\n- retries, cancellation, and resume logic need explicit job state rather than transient in-memory state\\n\\nThe durable model should include job records, segment records, asset metadata, and an event log that can reconstruct session progress after interruption.\\n\\n## Intended Backend Shape\\n\\nThe target backend should separate concerns explicitly:\\n\\n- `api/`: HTTP routes and websocket endpoints\\n- `settings/`: environment and secrets loading\\n- `db/`: database engine, migrations, and persistence setup\\n- `models/`: typed domain models and persistence schemas\\n- `repositories/`: database access for sessions, events, jobs, and assets\\n- `services/`: workflow logic and policy enforcement\\n- `ai/`: Gemini planning, composition, and narration adapters\\n- `worker/`: background job execution for writing and audio\\n- `storage/`: object storage abstraction over the local GCS emulator\\n\\n## Durable Domain Concepts\\n\\nThe system should persist the following concepts as first-class records:\\n\\n- Session\\n- Workflow stage\\n- Event log\\n- Selected genre\\n- Selected tone\\n- Story brief\\n- Pitch batch and selected pitch\\n- Character batch and selected character sheet\\n- Beat sheet\\n- Story setup preferences\\n- Outline or chapter plan\\n- Continuity bible\\n- Composition job and segments\\n- Audio job and segments\\n- Asset metadata\\n\\n## Cn.md\\\\nprompts/84-reader-ui-with-navigation.md\\\\nprompts/59-planning-funnel-e2e-test.md\\\\nprompts/07-developer-bootstrap-scripts.yolopilot.md\\\\nscripts/reset-local-data.sh\\\\nscripts/check-secret-hygiene.sh\\\\nscripts/dev-compose.sh\\\\nscripts/README.md\\\\nscripts/install-git-hooks.sh\\\\nscripts/bootstrap-dev.sh\\\\nREADME.md\\\\ndocs/architecture-overview.md\\\\ndocs/contributing.md\\\\ndocs/product-brief.md\\\\ndocs/README.md\\\\ndocs/secrets-and-local-config.md\\\\ndocs/adr/README.md\\\\nbackend/README.md\\\\nbackend/tests/test_settings.py\\\\nbackend/tests/test_health.py\\\\nbackend/tests/conftest.py\\\\nbackend/pyproject.toml\\\\nbackend/Dockerfile\\\\nbackend/requirements.txt\\\\nbackend/app/__init__.py\\\\nbackend/app/main.py\\\\nbackend/app/__main__.py\\\\nMakefile\\\\nbackend/app/services/__init__.py\\\\nbackend/app/services/health.py\\\\nbackend/migrations/README.md\\\\nbackend/app/models/__init__.py\\\\nbackend/app/models/system.py\\\\nbackend/app/settings/__init__.py\\\\nbackend/app/settings/config.py\\\\nbackend/app/storage/status.py\\\\nbackend/app/storage/__init__.py\\\\nbackend/app/api/__init__.py\\\\nbackend/app/api/router.py\\\\ntest-assets/README.md\\\\nfrontend/tsconfig.app.json\\\\nfrontend/index.html\\\\nfrontend/tsconfig.node.json\\\\nfrontend/README.md\\\\nfrontend/Dockerfile\\\\nbackend/app/worker/README.md\\\\nfrontend/tsconfig.json\\\\nfrontend/package.json\\\\nfrontend/package-lock.json\\\\nfrontend/vite.config.ts\\\\nfrontend/eslint.config.js\\\\nfrontend/vitest.config.ts\\\\ntools/README.md\\\\nfrontend/public/favicon.svg\\\\nbackend/app/api/v1/__init__.py\\\\nbackend/app/api/v1/router.py\\\\nbackend/app/api/routes/__init__.py\\\\nbackend/app/api/routes/health.py\\\\nbackend/app/api/routes/legacy.py\\\\nbackend/app/db/__init__.py\\\\nbackend/app/db/status.py\\\\nfrontend/src/vite-env.d.ts\\\\nbackend/app/api/v1/routes/__init__.py\\\\nbackend/app/api/v1/routes/health.py\\\\ninfra/persistence/README.md\\\\ninfra/README.md\\\\nfrontend/src/main.tsx\\\\ntools/webapp-qa/package-lock.json\\\\ntools/webapp-qa/Dockerfile\\\\ntools/webapp-qa/package.json\\\\ninfra/compose/docker-compose.yml\\\\ninfra/compose/README.md\\\\nfrontend/src/styles/index.css\\\\nfrontend/src/app/router.tsx\\\\nfrontend/src/app/AppShell.tsx\\\\nfrontend/src/app/App.tsx\\\\ntools/webapp-qa/scripts/run-spec.mjs\\\\nfrontend/src/test/setup.ts\\\\ntools/webapp-qa/examples/homepage.spec.json\\\\nfrontend/src/features/home/HomeRoute.test.tsx\\\\nfrontend/src/features/system/useBackendStatus.ts\\\\nfrontend/src/features/home/HomeRoute.tsx\\\\nfrontend/src/shared/api.ts\\\\n\\\", \\\"item.completed\\\"}, \\\"task_display_context\\\": {\\\"event_number\\\": 12, \\\"prompt_index\\\": 10, \\\"prompt_name\\\": \\\"09 \\\\u2014 Initial System Diagram and First Architecture Decisions\\\", \\\"prompt_total\\\": 100}, \\\"timestap\\\": \\\"2026-03-31T22:15:13-07:00\\\"}\\nprompts/10-domain-model-and-state-machine.yolopilot.md:669:    ./prompts/09-initial-system-diagram-and-adr.yolopilot.jsonlines:17:{\\\"color\\\": \\\"cyan\\\", \\\"emoji\\\": \\\"\\\\ud83d\\\\udee0\\\\ufe0f\\\", \\\"event_type\\\": \\\"Command Finished\\\", \\\"message\\\": \\\"completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,240p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\\\\\"\\\", \\\"raw_event\\\": {\\\"item\\\": {\\\"aggregated_output\\\": \\\"# Base Prompt \\\\u2014 Build the Bedtime Story + Audio App\\\\n\\\\nYou are building a production-minded local-first application that helps a user create a bedtime story from idea to finished text and finished narration audio.\\\\n\\\\nThis prompt pack is designed to be used **sequentially**. Read this file first, then work through the numbered prompts in order. Each numbered prompt should be treated as an incremental implementation task inside the same repository. Preserve prior work unless the new prompt explicitly asks you to refactor it.\\\\n\\\\n## Product Summary\\\\n\\\\nThe product is a guided story-creation studio with a persistent session model. A user should be able to:\\\\n\\\\n1. See past story sessions first, including both in-progress and completed work.\\\\n2. Open a session to resume, edit, or finalize it.\\\\n3. Use a **two-pane workspace**:\\\\n   - **Left pane:** chat window, about one-third of the desktop width.\\\\n   - **Main pane:** structured workflow UI, about two-thirds of the desktop width.\\\\n4. Control the product in **both directions**:\\\\n   - The user can type in chat, and the system can translate those messages into UI actions.\\\\n   - The user can act directly in the UI, and those actions should be reflected in the chat log and in durable session state.\\\\n5. Move through a staged workflow:\\\\n   - Genre selection\\\\n   - Tone selection\\\\n   - Story setup / free-form brief\\\\n   - Story pitches\\\\n   - Character sheet\\\\n   - Save-the-Cat beat sheet\\\\n   - Story setup preferences such as word count, runtime, and chapters\\\\n   - Composition\\\\n   - Audio configuration and audio generation\\\\n   - Finalize / read / listen / download\\\\n6. Download the final story as a **Word document** and the final narration as an audio file.\\\\n7. Read or listen inside the product UI after generation is complete.\\\\n\\\\n## Story-Generation Philosophy\\\\n\\\\nThe app is for **bedtime stories**, not generic fiction generation. That means:\\\\n\\\\n- Wonder is welcome.\\\\n- Adventure is welcome.\\\\n- Mystery is welcome.\\\\n- Tension is allowed, but it should be modulated for bedtime use.\\\\n- Emotional repair matters.\\\\n- The ending should feel safe, satisfying, and restful.\\\\n- The narration and UI tone should remain calm, readable, and trustworthy.\\\\n\\\\nUse the research docs in this prompt pack as direct product input, especially the Save The Cat notes and the curated genre/tone catalog.\\\\n\\\\n## Required Story Workflow\\\\n\\\\nThe session should support this exact conceptual flow:\\\\n\\\\n1. **Genre Selection**\\\\n   - The user selects from the curated genre list.\\\\n2. **Tone Selection**\\\\n   - The user is shown preconfigured tone options for the chosen genre.\\\\n3. **Story Setup**\\\\n   - The user provides a free-form description of the story they want to tell.\\\\n4. **Story Pitches**\\\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate pitches.\\\\n   - The user can select one, ask for alternatives, or refine via chat.\\\\n5. **Character Sheet**\\\\n   - Gemini generates multiple candidate character sheets.\\\\n   - The user can select or refine via chat.\\\\n6. **Story Beats**\\\\n   - Gemini generates a Save-the-Cat beat sheet for the selected plan.\\\\n   - The user can refine the beats via chat or UI.\\\\n7. **Story Setup Preferences**\\\\n   - The user can set soft planning targets such as word count, read-aloud duration, and chapter organization.\\\\n   - These are **guides**, not strict constraints.\\\\n8. **Composition**\\\\n   - The system writes in segments.\\\\n   - The UI shows a live typewriter-like experience.\\\\n   - Writing should generally appear faster than the user can comfortably read.\\\\n   - A visible progress bar should show overall story progress.\\\\n   - The agent should periodically post chat summaries of what it is writing.\\\\n   - The user can interrupt and redirect composition, including causing rewrites of prior segments.\\\\n9. **Audio**\\\\n   - The user configures voice, music, speed, and related settings.\\\\n   - The system estimates final length heuristically.\\\\n   - Audio is generated in segments with visible progress.\\\\n10. **Finalize**\\\\n   - The user can read and listen in the UI.\\\\n   - The user can download the story as a Word document and the final audio as a file.\\\\n\\\\n## Non-Negotiable Technical Requirements\\\\n\\\\n### Frontend\\\\n- React\\\\n- Vite\\\\n- TypeScript\\\\n- The browser must **never** hold provider secrets\\\\n- Chat pane on the left, main pane on the right\\\\n- Past sessions screen must be the first meaningful screen\\\\n\\\\n### Backend\\\\n- Python\\\\n- FastAPI\\\\n- Durable backend-owned business logic\\\\n- Clear separation between route handlers, domain services, AI orchestration, and persistence\\\\n\\\\n### Data and Infrastructure\\\\n- PostgreSQL is the durable store for structured application state\\\\n- Use a **file-backed GCS emulator** for object/blob storage in local development\\\\n- The app must run with Docker Compose\\\\n- Postgres and object storage must both be **persistent across restarts**\\\\n- `secrets.yaml` exists locally for API keys and must **not** be committed to git\\\\n\\\\n### AI\\\\n- Use the **Gemini 3.1 family** for text/planning/composition workloads\\\\n- Keep model IDs centralized in config, not scattered through the codebase\\\\n- Put AI calls behind backend services and adapters\\\\n- Use structured outputs wherever determinism matters\\\\n- Use separate adapters for:\\\\n  - planning/classification/intent parsing\\\\n  - long-form story generation\\\\n  - narration / TTS\\\\n- Keep narration generation isolated behind a provider-style interface because model offerings evolve\\\\n\\\\n## Recommended Architecture\\\\n\\\\nUse a calm, maintainable architecture rather than a clever one.\\\\n\\\\n### Suggested application shape\\\\n- `frontend/` \\\\u2014 React + Vite app\\\\n- `backend/` \\\\u2014 FastAPI app plus domain services\\\\n- `infra/` \\\\u2014 Docker Compose, Dockerfiles, local infrastructure helpers\\\\n- `docs/` \\\\u2014 architecture notes, ADRs, product docs\\\\n\\\\n### Suggested backend layers\\\\n- `api/` or route handlers\\\\n- `settings/`\\\\n- `db/`\\\\n- `models/`\\\\n- `repositories/`\\\\n- `services/`\\\\n- `worker/`\\\\n- `ai/`\\\\n- `storage/`\\\\n\\\\n### Suggested durable concepts\\\\n- Session\\\\n- Workflow stage\\\\n- Event log\\\\n- Selected genre\\\\n- Selected tone\\\\n- Story brief\\\\n- Pitch batch + selected pitch\\\\n- Character batch + selected character sheet\\\\n- Beat sheet\\\\n- Story setup preferences\\\\n- Outline / chapter or scene plan\\\\n- Continuity bible\\\\n- Composition job\\\\n- Composition segment\\\\n- Audio job\\\\n- Asset record\\\\n\\\\n## Real-Time and Long-Running Work\\\\n\\\\nTreat composition and audio generation as **durable background jobs**, not request-thread work.\\\\n\\\\n- The browser should be able to refresh without losing the job.\\\\n- The UI should receive live progress updates.\\\\n- The backend should own current truth about progress.\\\\n- A separate worker process is strongly preferred.\\\\n- Use PostgreSQL-backed durable job records.\\\\n- Persist partial outputs frequently enough that resume is trustworthy.\\\\n\\\\n## Chat and UI Bridge Rules\\\\n\\\\nThe product must support true bidirectional interaction.\\\\n\\\\n### Chat-to-UI\\\\nA user message can propose actions like:\\\\n- select a tone\\\\n- regenerate pitches\\\\n- choose a pitch\\\\n- adjust character traits\\\\n- soften the midpoint\\\\n- shorten the story target\\\\n- pause writing\\\\n- change narration speed\\\\n\\\\n### UI-to-Chat\\\\nA direct UI action should appear in the chat history as a compact structured summary, for example:\\\\n- \\\\u201cSelected genre: Quest Fantasy\\\\u201d\\\\n- \\\\u201cUpdated target runtime: ~12 minutes\\\\u201d\\\\n- \\\\u201cAccepted revised beat sheet\\\\u201d\\\\n\\\\n### Safety rule\\\\nThe model can **propose** actions, but a deterministic policy layer should decide whether they are valid in the current session state.\\\\n\\\\n## Save The Cat Expectations\\\\n\\\\nUse the Save The Cat method as the backbone of planning.\\\\n\\\\n- The beat sheet should be explicit, editable, and stored as structured data.\\\\n- The composition stage should write against the beat sheet and the detailed outline derived from it.\\\\n- Bedtime adaptation matters: the low points can be emotionally meaningful without becoming harsh or distressing.\\\\n- The final story should still feel like a transformation story, not just a string of nice scenes.\\\\n\\\\n## Genre and Tone Expectations\\\\n\\\\nUse the included research docs to seed:\\\\n- a curated genre list\\\\n- per-genre tone options\\\\n- bedtime-suitable arc notes\\\\n- tone descriptors that can guide UI copy and AI prompting\\\\n\\\\nDo **not** label tones with living or copyrighted-author names. Use generic tone labels and descriptors.\\\\n\\\\n## Audio Expectations\\\\n\\\\nNarration must be treated as a real product feature, not a side demo.\\\\n\\\\n- Segment audio generation so it is resumable\\\\n- Estimate length heuristically before generation\\\\n- Support voice selection\\\\n- Support speed controls\\\\n- Support optional background music\\\\n- Keep music subordinate to narration clarity\\\\n- Produce a final compiled audio artifact\\\\n- Keep intermediate and final artifacts trackable through durable metadata\\\\n\\\\n## Export Expectations\\\\n\\\\nAt minimum, the user must be able to get:\\\\n- final story text in the app\\\\n- final audio in the app\\\\n- downloadable `.docx`\\\\n- downloadable final audio file\\\\n\\\\n## Developer Experience and Quality Bar\\\\n\\\\nFavor:\\\\n- typed interfaces\\\\n- boring, explicit service boundaries\\\\n- migrations\\\\n- tests around durable state\\\\n- visible logs\\\\n- simple scripts for local dev\\\\n\\\", \\\"command\\\": \\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,240p' prompts/base_prompt.md\\\\\\\"\\\", \\\"exit_code\\\": 0, \\\"id\\\": \\\"item_6\\\", \\\"status\\\": \\\"completed\\\", \\\"type\\\": \\\"message\\\": \\\"completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/architecture-overview.md\\\\\\\"\\\", \\\"raw_event\\\": {\\\"item\\\": {\\\"aggregated_output\\\": \\\"# Architecture Overview\\\\n\\\\n## System Picture\\\\n\\\\nStoryteller is a full-stack application with a browser client, a backend-owned workflow engine, durable relational state, durable object storage, and resumable background jobs.\\\\n\\\\n```text\\\\nReact + Vite client\\\\n        |\\\\n        v\\\\nFastAPI API layer\\\\n        |\\\\n        +--> domain services and policy layer\\\\n        |         |\\\\n        |         +--> AI adapters for planning, composition, and narration\\\\n        |         |\\\\n        |         +--> repositories for sessions, workflow state, jobs, and event log\\\\n        |\\\\n        +--> background workers for composition and audio generation\\\\n                  |\\\\n                  +--> PostgreSQL for durable structured state\\\\n                  +--> file-backed GCS emulator for artifacts and intermediate blobs\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n## Why Gemini Calls Must Stay on the Backend\\\\n\\\\nAll Gemini access belongs on the server side for four reasons:\\\\n\\\\n1. Secrets control: provider credentials live in local `secrets.yaml` and must never enter the browser.\\\\n2. Policy enforcement: chat-to-UI actions and workflow transitions need deterministic server validation before the model can change session state.\\\\n3. Durable orchestration: prompts, structured outputs, retries, and model selection need to be coordinated with persistent session data and job records.\\\\n4. Provider flexibility: keeping Gemini behind backend adapters lets the app keep a stable internal interface while model IDs, structured output tactics, or narration providers evolve.\\\\n\\\\nThe frontend should only call the backend's API and subscribe to backend-owned progress events.\\\\n\\\\n## Why Composition and Audio Must Use Resumable Server-Side Jobs\\\\n\\\\nComposition and narration are long-running operations that cannot safely live inside request threads or browser memory. They need server-side jobs because:\\\\n\\\\n- a user must be able to refresh the page or return later without losing progress\\\\n- partial story text and audio segments need durable checkpoints\\\\n- progress updates need a single source of truth that both chat and UI can observe\\\\n- retries, cancellation, and resume logic need explicit job state rather than transient in-memory state\\\\n\\\\nThe durable model should include job records, segment records, asset metadata, and an event log that can reconstruct session progress after interruption.\\\\n\\\\n## Intended Backend Shape\\\\n\\\\nThe target backend should separate concerns explicitly:\\\\n\\\\n- `api/`: HTTP routes and websocket endpoints\\\\n- `settings/`: environment and secrets loading\\\\n- `db/`: database engine, migrations, and persistence setup\\\\n- `models/`: typed domain models and persistence schemas\\\\n- `repositories/`: database access for sessions, events, jobs, and assets\\\\n- `services/`: workflow logic and policy enforcement\\\\n- `ai/`: Gemini planning, composition, and narration adapters\\\\n- `worker/`: background job execution for writing and audio\\\\n- `storage/`: object storage abstraction over the local GCS emulator\\\\n\\\\n## Durable Domain Concepts\\\\n\\\\nThe system should persist the following concepts as first-class records:\\\\n\\\\n- Session\\\\n- Workflow stage\\\\n- Event log\\\\n- Selected genre\\\\n- Selected tone\\\\n- Story brief\\\\n- Pitch batch and selected pitch\\\\n- Character batch and selected character sheet\\\\n- Beat sheet\\\\n- Story setup preferences\\\\n- Outline or chapter plan\\\\n- Continuity bible\\\\n- Composition job and segments\\\\n- Audio job and segments\\\\n- Asset metadata\\\\n\\\\n## Current Repository State\\\\n\\\\nThe repository currently contains an initial scaffold:\\\\n\\\\n- `frontend/`: Vite React TypeScript app\\\\n- `backend/`: FastAPI app with an app factory, versioned routers, settings loader, models, services, and database placeholders\\\\n- `backend/migrations/`: reserved home for database schema history\\\\n- `backend/app/worker/`: reserved home for background job runners\\\\n- `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`: local frontend, backend, PostgreSQL, file-backed GCS emulator, and browser QA services\\\\n- `infra/persistence/`: notes for persistent local infrastructure data\\\\n- `scripts/dev-compose.sh`: convenience entrypoint for the Compose stack\\\\n- `tools/webapp-qa/`: containerized browser automation support\\\\n\\\\nBackground workers and most domain layers are still planned work. PostgreSQL and the file-backed GCS emulator are now available in the local Compose stack so later prompts can connect durable state and artifact storage without redefining the infrastructure contract.\\\\n\\\", \\\"command\\\": \\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/architecture-overview.md\\\\\\\"\\\", \\\"exit_code\\\": 0, \\\"id\\\": \\\"item_7\\\", \\\"status\\\": \\\"completed\\\", \\\"type\\\": \\\"command_execution\\\"}, \\\"type\\\": \\\"item.completed\\\"}, \\\"task_display_context\\\": {\\\"event_number\\\": 19, \\\"prompt_index\\\": 10, \\\"prompt_name\\\": \\\"09 \\\\u2014 Initial System Diagram and First Architecture Decisions\\\", \\\"prompt_total\\\": 100}, \\\"timestap\\\": \\\"2026-03-31T22:15:20-07:00\\\"}\\nprompts/10-domain-model-and-state-machine.yolopilot.md:671:    ./prompts/09-initial-system-diagram-and-adr.yolopilot.jsonlines:23:{\\\"color\\\": \\\"cyan\\\", \\\"emoji\\\": \\\"\\\\ud83d\\\\udee0\\\\ufe0f\\\", \\\"event_type\\\": \\\"Command Finished\\\", \\\"message\\\": \\\"completed, exit 0: /bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/product-brief.md\\\\\\\"\\\", \\\"raw_event\\\": {\\\"item\\\": {\\\"aggregated_output\\\": \\\"# Product Brief\\\\n\\\\n## Product Summary\\\\n\\\\nStoryteller is a session-based bedtime story creation studio. It helps a user move from a loose story idea to a finished written story and finished narration audio, with the ability to leave and come back later without losing progress.\\\\n\\\\nThe first meaningful screen is the sessions home. It must foreground past sessions, including both in-progress and completed stories, so resume and revisit are the default behaviors rather than an afterthought.\\\\n\\\\n## User Experience Principles\\\\n\\\\n- Bedtime-first: stories should support calm endings, emotional repair, and low-friction read-aloud use.\\\\n- Guided, not opaque: users move through explicit workflow stages instead of one giant prompt box.\\\\n- Bidirectional control: chat can suggest UI actions, and UI actions must be echoed back into chat.\\\\n- Durable by default: progress survives refreshes, restarts, and long-running generation work.\\\\n- Local-first development: the full stack should run locally through Docker Compose with persistent infrastructure.\\\\n\\\\n## End-to-End Workflow\\\\n\\\\nThe product must support this ordered flow:\\\\n\\\\n1. Genre selection from a curated genre list.\\\\n2. Tone selection from options preconfigured for the chosen genre.\\\\n3. Story setup through a free-form brief describing the desired story.\\\\n4. Story pitches generated by Gemini, with selection, refinement, or regeneration.\\\\n5. Character sheet generation, again with selection and refinement.\\\\n6. Save-the-Cat beat sheet generation and editing.\\\\n7. Story setup preferences for soft targets such as word count, runtime, and chapters.\\\\n8. Composition in segments with visible progress, typewriter-like output, and interruptibility.\\\\n9. Audio configuration and audio generation, including voice and speed controls plus optional background music.\\\\n10. Finalize, where the user can read, listen, and download story and audio artifacts.\\\\n\\\\n## Core Product Behaviors\\\\n\\\\n### Sessions and Resume\\\\n\\\\n- Sessions are durable backend entities.\\\\n- A user can reopen a session from any stage.\\\\n- In-progress and completed sessions both appear on the home screen.\\\\n\\\\n### Workspace Layout\\\\n\\\\n- Desktop layout uses two panes.\\\\n- Left pane is the chat workspace, about one-third width.\\\\n- Right pane is the main workflow UI, about two-thirds width.\\\\n\\\\n### Chat and UI Bridge\\\\n\\\\n- User chat can propose structured actions such as selecting a tone or refining beats.\\\\n- The model may propose actions, but a deterministic backend policy layer decides whether each action is valid for the session's current stage.\\\\n- Direct UI actions must create compact chat-visible summaries for auditability and replay.\\\\n\\\\n### Composition Experience\\\\n\\\\n- Story writing happens in segments.\\\\n- Progress is visible and should feel meaningfully live.\\\\n- The system should periodically summarize what it is writing in chat.\\\\n- Users can interrupt, redirect, and request rewrites.\\\\n\\\\n### Audio Experience\\\\n\\\\n- Narration is a first-class feature, not a demo extra.\\\\n- Audio generation is segmented and resumable.\\\\n- The app estimates likely runtime before generation starts.\\\\n- Final audio should be playable in-app and downloadable.\\\\n\\\\n## Non-Negotiable Technical Constraints\\\\n\\\\n- Frontend: React, Vite, TypeScript\\\\n- Backend: Python, FastAPI\\\\n- Structured persistence: PostgreSQL\\\\n- Object storage in local development: file-backed GCS emulator\\\\n- Local orchestration: Docker Compose\\\\n- Secrets: local `secrets.yaml`, excluded from git\\\\n- AI model family: Gemini 3.1, with backend-owned adapters and centralized model configuration\\\\n\\\\n## Deliverable Expectations\\\\n\\\\nThe finished product should let a user:\\\\n\\\\n- create and revise a bedtime story plan\\\\n- generate and edit a complete story\\\\n- generate, play, and download narration audio\\\\n- download the story as `.docx`\\\\n- resume work from prior sessions without losing context\\\\n\\\", \\\"command\\\": \\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/product-brief.md\\\\\\\"\\\", \\\"exit_code\\\": 0, \\\"id\\\": \\\"item_9\\\", \\\"status\\\": \\\"completed\\\", \\\"type\\\": \\\"command_execution\\\"}, \\\"type\\\": \\\"item.completed\\\"}, \\\"task_display_context\\\": {\\\"event_number\\\": 23,
